title: Amyema longipes
intro: Amyema longipes, also known as the Green-stemmed Mistletoe, is a hemiparasitic plant that belongs to the family Loranthaceae. It is traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Amyema longipes include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Amyema longipes is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or made into a paste or salve for topical application.
taxonomy: Amyema longipes is a species of parasitic flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae. It belongs to the order Santalales and is native to Australia. The plant is a hemiparasite, obtaining some nutrients from the soil but also deriving a significant amount from its host plant. At the genus level, Amyema longipes is a member of the tribe Loranthaceae tribe, which comprises a large number of similar parasitic plants. Classification of Amyema longipes places it within the kingdom Plantae.
